# IAutoGeneratedSaveManager

## Mental Model

Treat `IAutoGeneratedSaveManager` as an entry point or data node for this subsystem: inspect its properties first, then decide which methods to call.

**Namespace:** TaleWorlds.SaveSystem.Definition  
**Module:** TaleWorlds.SaveSystem  
**Type:** public interface

The **type-definition registration interface** of the save system. Implemented by the build-time-generated `AutoGeneratedSaveManager` to register save definitions for all built-in container types (`List<T>`, `Dictionary<,>`, etc.) into a `DefinitionContext`.

## Definition

```csharp
namespace TaleWorlds.SaveSystem.Definition
{
    public interface IAutoGeneratedSaveManager
    {
        void Initialize(DefinitionContext definitionContext);
    }
}
```

## Responsibility

- The save system must know how to serialize each container type. Maintaining these definitions by hand is impractical, so TaleWorlds generates `AutoGeneratedSaveManager` at compile time, implementing this interface.
- `Initialize` is called during `SaveManager.InitializeGlobalDefinitionContext`, injecting the generated container definitions into the global `DefinitionContext` in one pass.

## Usage example

```csharp
// Mods do not implement this interface; it is implemented by
// TaleWorlds auto-generated code. Knowing it exists explains why
// built-in collection types can be saved directly.
```

::: tip For mod developers
You do **not** need to, and should **not**, implement this interface. It is for engine auto-generated code only. If you define a custom generic container you want to save, use `[SaveableField]` / `[SaveableProperty]`, or register a type definition manually in `DefinitionContext`.
:::

## Usage Example

```csharp
IAutoGeneratedSaveManager implementation = GetAutoGeneratedSaveManagerImplementation();
```
